     The Numbers Business Report

The Rum Diary (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $13,109,815Details
International Box Office $8,434,917
Worldwide Box Office $21,544,732
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,035,374
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,418,822
Total $7,454,196
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Tired of the noise and madness of New York and the crushing conventions of late Eisenhower-era America, itinerant journalist Paul Kemp travels to the pristine island of Puerto Rico to write for a local San Juan newspaper run by the downtrodden editor Lotterman. Adopting the rum-soaked lifestyle of the late '50s version of Hemingway's "The Lost Generation," Paul soon becomes entangled with a very attractive American woman, Chenault and her fiancée Sanderson, a businessman involved in shady property development deals. It is within this world that Kemp ultimately discovers his true voice as a writer and integrity as a man.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,135,369 (39.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.55 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:60.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$45,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,272 opening theaters/2,292 max. theaters, 2.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $19,772,176

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 28th, 2011 (Wide) by FilmDistrict
International Releases: April 5th, 2012 (Wide) (Peru)
April 19th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
April 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Brazil)
April 24th, 2012 (Wide) (Italy)
May 4th, 2012 (Wide) (Spain)
Video Release: February 14th, 2012 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language, brief drug use and sexuality.
(Rating bulletin 2116, 4/14/2010)
Running Time: 122 minutes
Geographical setting: Puerto Rico
Plot point: Addiction, Fired, Infidelity, News, Poverty, Relationships Gone Wrong, White Collar Crime, Writing and Writers
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Infinitum Nihil, GK Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Tired of the noise and madness of New York and the crushing conventions of late Eisenhower-era America, itinerant journalist Paul Kemp travels to the pristine island of Puerto Rico to write for a local San Juan newspaper run by the downtrodden editor Lotterman. Adopting the rum-soaked lifestyle of the late '50s version of Hemingway's "The Lost Generation," Paul soon becomes entangled with a very attractive American woman, Chenault and her fiancée Sanderson, a businessman involved in shady property development deals. It is within this world that Kemp ultimately discovers his true voice as a writer and integrity as a man.
